title: pyrola japonica f. subaphylla
intro: Pyrola japonica f. subaphylla, also known as dwarf wintergreen, is a plant species in the heather family (Ericaceae) that is native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and acting as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine and as a dietary supplement.
